growth_records.strain: not specified (model calibrated to Kuhla and Oelze 1988b chemostat cultures)

yields.note: model fit parameters: energy-transfer efficiency epsilon=0.22, membrane O2 diffusion efficiency epsilon_m=7.9e-4; protein-C:total-biomass-C ratio = 1:1.32

growth_records.cite: Inomura et al. A quantitative analysis of the direct and indirect costs of nitrogen fixation: a model based on Azotobacter vinelandii. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.97. [PMC5315487]
growth_records.snippet: With growth rate of 1 per day, our Azotobacter study suggests a sweet spot of ~3% O2 saturation at which the organisms are neither oxygen-limited nor poisoned. ... At the lowest oxygen concentration (12 uM) there was a sharp change in the s
growth_records.notes: Modeling paper; quantitative sucrose-consumption fluxes (Cs vs D) are only in Figures 1/3/4 and not text-extractable as numbers, so uptake/secretion left empty. Growth-rate value taken from explicit text scenario mu=1 per day: mu_per_h = 1/24 = 0.0417 /h; doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/1 day = 0.693 day = 16.6 h; dilution_rate_per_h = D = mu at chemostat steady state = 1/day = 0.0417/h (0.00417 shown is the separately-stated transition D~0.1/day = 0.1/24 = 0.00417/h). Carbon source = sucrose (approximated as two glucose in model); nitrogen source = N2 (diazotrophic, N-free medium). O2 conditions 12-192 uM stated in text. Medium composition not given in this paper (derived from Kuhla and Oelze 1988b).

growth_records.medium: not stated (ammonium as N source; glucose C source)
growth_records.cite: Campos Diego Tec et al. Modeling of nitrogen fixation and polymer production in the heterotrophic diazotroph Azotobacter vinelandii DJ. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00132. [PMC7292883]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2. Predicted and experimental growth rates reported for A. vinelandii DJ under different carbon and nitrogen sources. Glucose | Ammonium | Experimental value 0.0505 1/h | Predicted 0.0486 | Clementi (1997).
growth_records.notes: A. vinelandii DJ growth on glucose + ammonium. Experimental mu=0.0505/h is cited FROM Clementi (1997), Crit Rev Biotechnol 17:327-361, not measured in this paper. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.0505=13.72 h (calculated). Temperature/pH not stated in this paper. No experimental uptake/secretion rates given for this condition (model uptake constraints were estimated, not measured).

growth_records.medium: not stated (ammonium as N source; mannitol C source)

up.method: uptake constraint estimated from experimental growth condition and set in model

growth_records.cite: Campos Diego Tec et al. Modeling of nitrogen fixation and polymer production in the heterotrophic diazotroph Azotobacter vinelandii DJ. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00132. [PMC7292883]
growth_records.snippet: the predicted growth using mannitol (uptake rate of 0.83 mmol/gDW/h) as sole carbon source was 0.0472 1/h, agreeing with the experimental data (0.045 +/- 0.003 1/h). Table 2: Mannitol | Ammonium | 0.045 +/- 0.003 | 0.0472 | Revin et al. (20
growth_records.notes: A. vinelandii DJ growth on mannitol + ammonium. Experimental mu=0.045+/-0.003 /h cited FROM Revin et al. (2018). td=0.693/0.045=15.40 h (calculated). Mannitol uptake 0.83 mmol/gDW/h is a MODEL constraint estimated from the experimental condition (not directly measured) -> is_experimental=false. Temp/pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: not stated (ammonium as N source; sucrose C source)
growth_records.cite: Campos Diego Tec et al. Modeling of nitrogen fixation and polymer production in the heterotrophic diazotroph Azotobacter vinelandii DJ. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00132. [PMC7292883]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2: Sucrose | Ammonium | 0.076 +/- 0.004 | 0.07 | Diaz-Barrera et al. (2016).
growth_records.notes: A. vinelandii DJ growth on sucrose + ammonium. Experimental mu=0.076+/-0.004 /h cited FROM Diaz-Barrera et al. (2016). td=0.693/0.076=9.12 h (calculated). Temp/pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: not stated (N2 as N source; glucose C source), diazotrophic
growth_records.cite: Campos Diego Tec et al. Modeling of nitrogen fixation and polymer production in the heterotrophic diazotroph Azotobacter vinelandii DJ. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00132. [PMC7292883]
growth_records.notes: A. vinelandii DJ diazotrophic growth on glucose with molecular N2 as N source. Experimental mu=0.06+/-0.0002 /h cited FROM Wong (1988). td=0.693/0.06=11.55 h (calculated). Nitrogen and H2 uptake were not fixed in the model. Temp/pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: not stated (N2 as N source; fructose C source), diazotrophic
growth_records.cite: Campos Diego Tec et al. Modeling of nitrogen fixation and polymer production in the heterotrophic diazotroph Azotobacter vinelandii DJ. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00132. [PMC7292883]
growth_records.notes: A. vinelandii DJ diazotrophic growth on fructose with molecular N2. Experimental mu=0.048+/-0.002 /h cited FROM Wong (1988). td=0.693/0.048=14.44 h (calculated). Temp/pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: not stated (N2 as N source; galactose C source), diazotrophic
growth_records.cite: Campos Diego Tec et al. Modeling of nitrogen fixation and polymer production in the heterotrophic diazotroph Azotobacter vinelandii DJ. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00132. [PMC7292883]
growth_records.notes: A. vinelandii DJ diazotrophic growth on galactose with molecular N2. Experimental mu=0.074+/-0.007 /h cited FROM Wong (1988). td=0.693/0.074=9.36 h (calculated). Temp/pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: not stated (ammonium as N source; glucose C source), alginate-production condition

up.method: uptake constraint estimated from experimental condition and set in model

growth_records.cite: Campos Diego Tec et al. Modeling of nitrogen fixation and polymer production in the heterotrophic diazotroph Azotobacter vinelandii DJ. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00132. [PMC7292883]
growth_records.snippet: the predicted growth using glucose (uptake rate of 0.33 mmol/gDW/h) as the sole carbon source was 0.1478 1/h and an alginate production rate of 0.25 mmol/gDW/h, agreeing with the experimental data (growth rate of 0.152 +/- 0.012 1/h and alg
growth_records.notes: A. vinelandii DJ alginate-production condition on glucose + ammonium. Experimental mu=0.152+/-0.012 /h and experimental alginate production rate 0.265+/-0.018 mmol/gDW/h, both cited FROM literature (Revin et al. 2018). td=0.693/0.152=4.56 h (calculated). Glucose uptake 0.33 mmol/gDW/h is a model constraint estimated from the experimental condition (is_experimental=false). Distinct from the glucose+ammonium record above (mu 0.0505, Clementi 1997) because this is a separate alginate-production growth study. Temp/pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: Modified Burk's medium (sucrose C-source, non-N-fixing / fixed-N)

sec.units: g g-1 h-1 (qAlg, specific alginate production rate)

growth_records.cite: García, Andres et al. Molecular weight and viscosifying power of alginates produced by mutant strains of Azotobacter vinelandii under microaerophilic conditions. Biotechnology Reports (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.btre.2020.e00436. [PMC7049565]
growth_records.snippet: OTRmax reached values of 4.8 ± 0.28 ... for ATCC 9046 ... qO2 13.6 ± 0.4 mmol O2 g_prot-1 h-1, RQ 1.10, μ 0.09 ± 0.02 h-1, maximal alginate 3.6 ± 0.6 g L-1, qAlg 0.050, P3HB 58% (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 experimental. qO2 is specific O2 UPTAKE (mmol O2 per g PROTEIN per h, not gDW). RQ=CTR/OTR=1.10 (implies CO2 secretion rate CTR=RQ*OTRmax=1.10*4.8=5.28 mmol L-1 h-1 volumetric). Doubling time td=ln2/μ=0.693/0.09=7.70 h. P3HB 58% is intracellular polymer fraction not a rate. Alginate max 3.6 g/L is a titer; qAlg 0.050 g g-1 h-1 is the specific secretion rate. Xmax not tabulated for this strain.

growth_records.cite: García, Andres et al. Molecular weight and viscosifying power of alginates produced by mutant strains of Azotobacter vinelandii under microaerophilic conditions. Biotechnology Reports (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.btre.2020.e00436. [PMC7049565]
growth_records.snippet: OTRmax 5.24 ± 0.29 ... AT9 qO2 16.4 ± 0.2 mmol O2 g_prot-1 h-1 (25% higher than GG9/ATCC9046), RQ 1.05, μ 0.09 ± 0.017 h-1, alginate 3.8 ± 0.2 g L-1, qAlg 0.052, P3HB 39% (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Highest qO2 strain. qO2=16.4 mmol O2 g_prot-1 h-1 (specific O2 uptake). RQ 1.05 -> CTR(CO2)=1.05*5.2=5.46 mmol L-1 h-1 volumetric. td=0.693/0.09=7.70 h. Xmax(protein)=0.90 g L-1 for AT9 (text: OPAlgU+ and AT9 produced 0.80 and 0.90). Alginate 3.8 g/L titer; qAlg 0.052 specific secretion rate.

growth_records.cite: García, Andres et al. Molecular weight and viscosifying power of alginates produced by mutant strains of Azotobacter vinelandii under microaerophilic conditions. Biotechnology Reports (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.btre.2020.e00436. [PMC7049565]
growth_records.snippet: GG9 gave highest Xmax=1.15 ± 0.04 g L-1 and μ=0.10 ± 0.021 h-1; qO2 12.9 ± 0.3, RQ 1.13, alginate 2.6 ± 0.5 g L-1, qAlg 0.037, P3HB 60% (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Highest μ (0.10) and highest Xmax(protein)=1.15 g L-1. qO2=12.9 mmol O2 g_prot-1 h-1. RQ 1.13 (highest) -> CTR(CO2)=1.13*4.8=5.42 mmol L-1 h-1. td=0.693/0.10=6.93 h. High RQ associated with P3HB (60%) over alginate.

growth_records.cite: García, Andres et al. Molecular weight and viscosifying power of alginates produced by mutant strains of Azotobacter vinelandii under microaerophilic conditions. Biotechnology Reports (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.btre.2020.e00436. [PMC7049565]
growth_records.snippet: OPAlgU+ OTRmax 5.23 ± 0.18, qO2 15.2 ± 0.25 mmol O2 g_prot-1 h-1, RQ 1.06, μ 0.08 ± 0.015 h-1, alginate 2.8 ± 0.2 g L-1, qAlg 0.046, P3HB 61% (Table 1); Xmax(protein)=0.80 g L-1
growth_records.notes: qO2=15.2 mmol O2 g_prot-1 h-1. RQ 1.06 -> CTR(CO2)=1.06*5.2=5.51 mmol L-1 h-1. td=0.693/0.08=8.66 h. Xmax(protein)=0.80 g L-1 (text). Alginate 2.8 g/L titer, qAlg 0.046 specific secretion rate.
